title: JWT Decoder: Decode, Inspect & Verify JSON Web Tokens Online
description: Why do most JWT bugs slip into production? Decode your payload, inspect expiration claims, and uncover the hidden structure before it breaks your app.

mainEntity.name: What is a JWT decoder and how does it work?

acceptedAnswer.text: A JWT decoder is a tool that takes a base64url-encoded JSON Web Token and breaks it apart into its three components: header, payload, and signature. It decodes the base64url strings into readable JSON so you can inspect the data inside the token, such as user claims and expiration dates.

mainEntity.name: How to decode a JWT token?

acceptedAnswer.text: To decode a JWT token, you simply paste the token string (which looks like xxxxx.yyyyy.zzzzz) into a JWT debugger. The tool splits the token by the dots (.) and decodes the first two parts (header and payload) from base64url format into plain JSON.

mainEntity.name: Is this a secure jwt.io alternative?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, this tool is a secure jwt.io alternative. It runs entirely within your browser using client-side JavaScript. Your tokens are never transmitted to any server. You can even load the page, disconnect from the internet, and continue to use the tool completely offline.

mainEntity.name: Can I decode a JWT without a secret?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you can decode the header and payload of a JWT without knowing the secret key. The data in a JWT is only encoded (using base64url), not encrypted. However, you cannot verify the signature or ensure the token hasn't been tampered with unless you have the correct secret or public key.

mainEntity.name: Why is my JWT signature invalid in the jwt debugger?

acceptedAnswer.text: An invalid signature usually means one of three things: the token data (header or payload) was modified after it was signed, you are using the wrong secret/public key to verify it, or you selected the wrong algorithm. Ensure your secret matches exactly what the server uses.
